questions: A well-known early-2000s beginner UNIX/Linux tutorial created at a UK university is released under a Creative Commons license whose canonical URL ends with “/by-nc-sa/2.0/”. Identify this specific license version and comprehensively describe: (1) the freedoms it grants to users, (2) all core conditions imposed on reuse and redistribution, (3) any statements regarding the irrevocability of the granted freedoms, (4) the notice about how this version compares to newer 4.0 licenses, and (5) the disclaimers or limitations related to warranties and other rights. Your answer should synthesize information from the official license deed and associated explanatory notices.

A widely used open-content license allows users to copy, redistribute, remix, transform, and build upon a work, provided they give appropriate credit, do not use it for commercial purposes, and license derivative works under identical terms. It also prohibits applying additional legal or technological restrictions, clarifies that no warranties are given, and notes that users need not comply for material in the public domain or where exceptions apply. An older 2.0 version of this license includes a canonical URL and a notice recommending adoption of a newer 4.0 version for improved international robustness. Identify this specific license and comprehensively describe (1) the freedoms it grants, (2) the conditions and restrictions it imposes, (3) the disclaimer and public domain exception language, and (4) the recommendation regarding newer versions, including the canonical reference associated with the 2.0 version.

An older open educational resource about UNIX and Linux is distributed under a Creative Commons license and includes a notice recommending that creators instead use the newer 4.0 versions of Creative Commons licenses. Focusing only on the 4.0 suite, identify the two Attribution-based licenses where (a) one permits commercial reuse without a ShareAlike requirement and (b) the other prohibits commercial use and requires ShareAlike. For both licenses, provide their canonical URLs and summarize their core permissions and obligations. Then, drawing from official Creative Commons documentation about version 4.0, explain at least four major improvements introduced in the 4.0 license suite compared to prior versions, including aspects related to international enforceability, treatment of database rights, attribution flexibility, moral/publicity rights, and the reinstatement mechanism for license violations.

You are preparing to publish a "UNIX / Linux Tutorial for Beginners" and want to release it under the most up-to-date, internationally robust open license version recommended by the nonprofit organization that maintains a suite of widely used public copyright licenses. Identify this nonprofit organization and explain its core mission. Then, compare the three distinct 10-week, sector-specific certificate courses it offers (including their intended audiences and learning focus), and describe the available bootcamp training formats and participation structure. Additionally, outline the developer-oriented tools and open source projects this organization provides to help integrate and manage its licenses in software platforms. Finally, reference the short anniversary video celebrating roughly twenty years of the organization’s work by naming its creators and specifying the license under which the video is released.

An author of a beginner-friendly UNIX/Linux tutorial wants to release their material under a Creative Commons 4.0 license. In one scenario, they want to allow anyone to use, modify, and redistribute the work for any purpose, including commercially, as long as proper credit is given and no additional legal restrictions are imposed. In another scenario, they want to allow sharing and adaptation, but prohibit commercial use and require that derivative works be distributed under the same license terms. 

Identify the two specific Creative Commons 4.0 licenses that satisfy these two different goals. For each license, provide: (1) its canonical URL, (2) the core permissions granted, (3) the key conditions or restrictions imposed, and (4) a summary of important improvements introduced in the 4.0 license suite that make these licenses more internationally robust and user-friendly compared to earlier versions (such as changes related to global enforceability, database rights, attribution practices, moral rights, and reinstatement after violations).

rounds.question: A well-known early-2000s beginner UNIX/Linux tutorial created at a UK university is released under a Creative Commons license whose canonical URL ends with “/by-nc-sa/2.0/”. Identify this specific license version and comprehensively describe: (1) the freedoms it grants to users, (2) all core conditions imposed on reuse and redistribution, (3) any statements regarding the irrevocability of the granted freedoms, (4) the notice about how this version compares to newer 4.0 licenses, and (5) the disclaimers or limitations related to warranties and other rights. Your answer should synthesize information from the official license deed and associated explanatory notices.
checklist_width: Correct identification of the freedoms: Share (copy and redistribute) and Adapt (remix, transform, build upon)
Explicit mention that freedoms are irrevocable provided license terms are followed
Attribution requirement (credit, link to license, indicate changes, no implied endorsement)
NonCommercial restriction (no commercial use)
ShareAlike requirement (derivatives must use the same license)
No additional restrictions clause (no legal/technological barriers beyond license)
Statement that compliance is not required for public domain elements or applicable exceptions/limitations
Notice that this is an older version and that 4.0 licenses are more user-friendly and internationally robust, with recommendation to use 4.0 for new works
Disclaimer of warranties and note that other rights (e.g., privacy, publicity, moral rights) may limit use
checklist_depth: Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 2.0 (BY-NC-SA 2.0) + Logic proof: identified via canonical URL ending '/by-nc-sa/2.0/' and its use by an early-2000s UNIX tutorial under a Creative Commons license
rounds.rationale: The question uses contextual clues (an early-2000s UK UNIX tutorial and a canonical URL ending in '/by-nc-sa/2.0/') to require logical identification of the exact Creative Commons license (Deep reasoning). It then demands a comprehensive synthesis of freedoms, conditions, notices about version updates, and disclaimers drawn from multiple sections of the official license deed and explanatory pages (Wide aggregation).
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Notice This is an older version of this license. Compared to previous versions, the 4.0 versions of all CC licenses are more user-friendly and more internationally robust . If
rounds.answer_a:
Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 2.0 Generic (CC BY-NC-SA 2.0) is the license identified by the URL ending “/by-nc-sa/2.0/” [1].

1. Freedoms granted  
• Share – copy, distribute and transmit the material in any medium or format.  
• Adapt – remix, transform and build upon the material.  
These freedoms apply for any purpose except those restricted below, and are available worldwide and for the duration of applicable copyright [1].

2. Core conditions on reuse and redistribution  
• Attribution – you must give appropriate credit, provide a link to the license and indicate if changes were made, in a reasonable manner and without suggesting endorsement [1].  
• Non-Commercial – you may not use the material for commercial purposes [1].  
• ShareAlike – if you remix, transform or build upon the work, you must distribute your contributions under the same or a compatible license [1].  
• No Additional Restrictions – you may not apply legal terms or technological measures that legally restrict others from doing anything the license permits [1].

3. Irrevocability  
“The licensor cannot revoke these freedoms as long as you follow the license terms,” meaning the permissions are perpetual provided compliance is maintained [1].

4. Notice comparing to 4.0  
The deed notes that the 4.0 license suite is the most up-to-date version and encourages its use for new works, linking to the newer deeds for comparison [1].

5. Disclaimers and limitations  
• No warranties are given; the material is provided “as-is” without representations or warranties of any kind [1].  
• The license may not grant all permissions needed for your intended use—other rights such as publicity, privacy, or moral rights may still apply [1].  
• Use of public-domain elements or statutory exceptions is not affected—you need not comply with the license for those parts [1].

## References  
[1] Creative Commons. “Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 2.0 Generic (CC BY-NC-SA 2.0) Deed.” https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/2.0/
rounds.answer_b:
The tutorial is **UNIX Tutorial for Beginners** created at the **University of Surrey**, and the specific license is **Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 2.0 Generic (CC BY-NC-SA 2.0)** [1][2].

**(1) Freedoms Granted:** The license allows users to **copy and redistribute** the material in any medium or format, and to **remix, transform, and build upon** the material. These freedoms apply as long as the license conditions are followed [3].

**(2) Core Conditions:** Three conditions govern reuse and redistribution. **Attribution (BY):** Users must give appropriate credit, provide a link to the license, and indicate if changes were made, in any reasonable manner but not suggesting licensor endorsement. **NonCommercial (NC):** The material may not be used for commercial purposes. **ShareAlike (SA):** If users remix, transform, or build upon the material, they must distribute contributions under the same license as the original [3].

**(3) Irrevocability:** The licensor cannot revoke the freedoms granted as long as the user follows the license terms. This means once the license is granted, it remains effective and cannot be withdrawn retroactively [3].

**(4) Comparison to Newer Licenses:** The deed includes a notice stating that this is a **human-readable summary** of a previous version and that users should consider licensing under **CC BY-NC-SA 4.0**, which provides improvements including international applicability, better clarity, and more robust legal infrastructure. The 4.0 version is described as the most current and recommended version [3][4].

**(5) Disclaimers and Limitations:** The license deed explicitly states it comes with **no warranties**. It also notes that the license may not grant all permissions necessary for intended use, as other rights such as **publicity, privacy, or moral rights** may limit usage. The deed is not itself a license but a simplified summary of the full legal code [3].

## References
[1] University of Surrey UNIX Tutorial for Beginners - http://www.ee.surrey.ac.uk/Teaching/Unix/
[2] Creative Commons License Reference on UNIX Tutorial - http://www.ee.surrey.ac.uk/Teaching/Unix/
[3] CC BY-NC-SA 2.0 Deed - https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/2.0/
[4] Creative Commons License Versions - https://creativecommons.org/faq/#why-should-i-use-the-latest-version-of-the-creative-commons-licenses
rounds.judge_reasoning: Both agents correctly identified the license as Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 2.0 (CC BY-NC-SA 2.0), satisfying the Deep Logic requirement. However, on Width/Completeness, Agent A covers all checklist elements: Share and Adapt freedoms, irrevocability, Attribution, NonCommercial, ShareAlike, No Additional Restrictions, the public domain/exceptions carve-out, the 4.0 upgrade notice, and warranty/other-rights disclaimers. Agent B omits two required elements: the explicit 'No Additional Restrictions' clause and the statement that compliance is not required for public domain material or statutory exceptions. While Agent B has slightly stronger visual formatting and contextual detail, Agent A is more complete and fully aligned with the ground-truth checklist. Therefore, Agent A provides the better overall answer.

lines: -> WINNER is A. Targeting Loser Failure: WIDE.

rounds.question: A widely used open-content license allows users to copy, redistribute, remix, transform, and build upon a work, provided they give appropriate credit, do not use it for commercial purposes, and license derivative works under identical terms. It also prohibits applying additional legal or technological restrictions, clarifies that no warranties are given, and notes that users need not comply for material in the public domain or where exceptions apply. An older 2.0 version of this license includes a canonical URL and a notice recommending adoption of a newer 4.0 version for improved international robustness. Identify this specific license and comprehensively describe (1) the freedoms it grants, (2) the conditions and restrictions it imposes, (3) the disclaimer and public domain exception language, and (4) the recommendation regarding newer versions, including the canonical reference associated with the 2.0 version.
checklist_width: Freedom to Share — copy and redistribute in any medium or format
Freedom to Adapt — remix, transform, and build upon the material
Attribution requirement — give appropriate credit, link to license, indicate changes
NonCommercial restriction — no commercial use permitted
ShareAlike requirement — derivatives must use the same license
No additional restrictions clause — no extra legal/technological barriers
Public domain/exception clause — compliance not required for public domain or legal exceptions
No warranties disclaimer

Canonical URL reference to the 2.0 license version

Recognition that it is an older 2.0 version with an official Creative Commons canonical URL and upgrade recommendation to 4.0
rounds.rationale: The question uses logical conditions (Attribution + NonCommercial + ShareAlike + no additional restrictions + 2.0 version notice) to implicitly identify the specific Creative Commons license without naming it (Deep reasoning). It then requires aggregating multiple categories of information—freedoms, conditions, disclaimers, public domain exceptions, version comparison, and canonical reference—drawn from the full license description (Wide scope).
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Notice This is an older version of this license. Compared to previous versions, the 4.0 versions of all CC licenses are more user-friendly and more internationally robust . If
rounds.answer_a:
Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 2.0 Generic (abbreviated “CC BY-NC-SA 2.0”)—canonical deed URL https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/2.0/—is the license described.  

1. Freedoms granted  
• Share: anyone may copy and redistribute the material in any medium or format.  
• Adapt: anyone may remix, transform, and build upon the material.  
These permissions apply worldwide and last for the entire term of copyright; “the licensor cannot revoke these freedoms as long as you follow the license terms.” [1]

2. Conditions and restrictions  
• Attribution – users must give appropriate credit, provide the license link, and indicate if changes were made, doing so in a reasonable way without implying endorsement.  
• NonCommercial – the material may not be used “for commercial purposes.”  
• ShareAlike – adaptations must be distributed under “the same or a compatible license.”  
• No additional restrictions – users may not impose legal terms or technological measures (e.g., DRM) that prevent others exercising the licensed freedoms. [1]

3. Disclaimer and public-domain/exception language  
The deed states that the material is supplied “as-is and as-available” with “no warranties… of any kind.” The license “may not give you all of the permissions necessary for your intended use,” and other rights (publicity, privacy, moral rights, etc.) may still apply. It further clarifies that users “do not have to comply with the license for elements of the material in the public domain or where your use is permitted by an applicable exception or limitation.” [1]

4. Recommendation regarding newer versions  
Because CC BY-NC-SA 2.0 predates the internationally harmonised 4.0 suite, the deed carries a prominent notice: “New! Read about how the license compares to the 4.0 version” and encourages creators to “consider using the 4.0 version” for new works, linking to the 4.0 deed for better global enforceability and interoperability. [1][2]

## References  
[1] Creative Commons. “Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 2.0 Generic Deed.” https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/2.0/  
[2] Creative Commons. “About the 4.0 License Suite.” https://creativecommons.org/version4
rounds.answer_b:
The license described is **Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 2.0 Generic (CC BY-NC-SA 2.0)**, with the canonical URL **https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/2.0/** [1]. This is the same license applied to the University of Surrey's UNIX Tutorial for Beginners [2].

## (1) Freedoms Granted

CC BY-NC-SA 2.0 grants users two fundamental freedoms: **Share** — the right to copy and redistribute the material in any medium or format — and **Adapt** — the right to remix, transform, and build upon the material. These freedoms apply for any non-commercial purpose. Critically, the licensor **cannot revoke** these freedoms as long as the licensee follows the license terms [1][3].

## (2) Conditions and Restrictions

Three core conditions apply. **Attribution (BY):** Users must give appropriate credit, provide a link to the license, and indicate if changes were made, in any reasonable manner but not in a way suggesting licensor endorsement. **NonCommercial (NC):** Users may not exploit the material for commercial purposes. **ShareAlike (SA):** Derivative works must be distributed under the same or a compatible license. Additionally, users may not apply **additional legal terms or technological measures** (such as DRM) that legally restrict others from exercising the rights the license grants [1][3].

## (3) Disclaimers and Public Domain Exceptions

The license provides **no warranties** of any kind. It notes that other rights — including publicity, privacy, and moral rights — may limit how the material is used. Importantly, users **need not comply** with the license for elements in the **public domain** or where an applicable **copyright exception or limitation** (such as fair use) applies [1][3].

## (4) Recommendation Regarding Newer Versions

The CC BY-NC-SA 2.0 deed includes an explicit notice that this is a **previous version** and recommends users consider licensing new works under **CC BY-NC-SA 4.0**. The 4.0 version offers improved international applicability, greater legal clarity, and enhanced robustness across jurisdictions. The canonical reference for the 2.0 version remains **https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/2.0/** [1][4].

## References
[1] CC BY-NC-SA 2.0 Deed — https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/2.0/
[2] University of Surrey UNIX Tutorial for Beginners — http://www.ee.surrey.ac.uk/Teaching/Unix/
[3] CC BY-NC-SA 2.0 Legal Code — https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/2.0/legalcode
[4] Creative Commons FAQ: Why should I use the latest version? — https://creativecommons.org/faq/#why-should-i-use-the-latest-version-of-the-creative-commons-licenses
rounds.judge_reasoning: Both agents correctly identify the license as Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 2.0 (CC BY-NC-SA 2.0), passing the DEEP logic check. Both comprehensively cover the required WIDTH elements: freedoms (share/adapt), attribution, noncommercial restriction, share-alike requirement, no additional restrictions clause, public domain/exception language, no-warranty disclaimer, recommendation to use 4.0, and the canonical 2.0 URL. There are no significant factual errors in either response. However, Agent B provides slightly superior user experience through clearer bold section headers, stronger scannability, more explicit emphasis on the ‘previous version’ notice, and higher citation density (including the legal code and FAQ). The content depth is equivalent, but B’s formatting and structure are more polished and search-engine-like. Therefore, B wins on presentation and overall utility, with no hard checklist failures by A.

lines: -> WINNER is B. Targeting Loser Failure: NONE.

rounds.question: An older open educational resource about UNIX and Linux is distributed under a Creative Commons license and includes a notice recommending that creators instead use the newer 4.0 versions of Creative Commons licenses. Focusing only on the 4.0 suite, identify the two Attribution-based licenses where (a) one permits commercial reuse without a ShareAlike requirement and (b) the other prohibits commercial use and requires ShareAlike. For both licenses, provide their canonical URLs and summarize their core permissions and obligations. Then, drawing from official Creative Commons documentation about version 4.0, explain at least four major improvements introduced in the 4.0 license suite compared to prior versions, including aspects related to international enforceability, treatment of database rights, attribution flexibility, moral/publicity rights, and the reinstatement mechanism for license violations.
checklist_width: Canonical URL for Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0)
Permissions of CC BY 4.0 (commercial sharing and adaptation allowed)
Obligations of CC BY 4.0 (attribution, no additional restrictions)
Canonical URL for Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International (CC BY-NC-SA 4.0)
Permissions of CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (sharing and adapting with NonCommercial restriction)
Obligations of CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (attribution, noncommercial, ShareAlike, no additional restrictions)
Explanation of 4.0 international robustness and removal of porting requirement
Explanation of explicit inclusion of sui generis database rights in 4.0
Explanation of improved attribution flexibility (e.g., linking to attribution page)
Explanation of waiver/clarification of moral, publicity, and personality rights
Explanation of 30-day automatic reinstatement after curing license violations
checklist_depth: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0) + Identified as the Attribution-only license that permits commercial use and adaptations without ShareAlike
Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International (CC BY-NC-SA 4.0) + Identified as the Attribution-based license that prohibits commercial use and requires ShareAlike
Reference to Creative Commons version 4.0 license suite documentation + Correctly tied to improvements over 3.0 and earlier versions
rounds.rationale: The question uses the contextual clue of an older UNIX/Linux educational resource recommending newer 4.0 licenses to indirectly anchor the search in Creative Commons licensing (Deep). It avoids naming the licenses directly and instead defines them by logical properties (commercial allowance vs. NonCommercial + ShareAlike). The respondent must then aggregate dispersed information from multiple official license deeds (two separate licenses) and from Creative Commons documentation describing the 4.0 improvements (Wide), requiring synthesis across several primary sources.
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Canonical URL https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/ See the legal code You are free to: Share — copy and redistribute the material in any medium or format for any purpo
rounds.answer_a:
Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0) – canonical URL https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/ – lets anyone  
• Share – copy and redistribute the material in any medium or format,  
• Adapt – remix, transform and build upon it for any purpose, including commercially.  
Obligations: give appropriate credit, provide the license link, indicate changes, and avoid additional legal/technological restrictions. No ShareAlike or non-commercial requirement applies [1].

Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International (CC BY-NC-SA 4.0) – canonical URL https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/ – permits the same sharing and adapting, but only for non-commercial purposes. Derivative works must be licensed under identical or compatible terms. Users must also supply attribution and refrain from extra restrictions [2].

Major improvements introduced with the 4.0 license suite:

1. Global, un-ported text: 4.0 is drafted as a single international license, eliminating the need for dozens of jurisdiction-specific “ports” and improving cross-border enforceability and certainty for licensors and users [2][3].

2. Explicit coverage of sui generis database rights: Section 4 extends the licensed permissions to any database rights the licensor holds, ensuring uniform treatment of data in regions (e.g., the EU) that recognise such rights and preventing a separate layer of permission from being required [3].

3. More flexible attribution: 4.0 allows attribution “in any reasonable manner,” including by hyperlink or uniform resource identifier, and permits aggregating credits in one place (such as a webpage or slide) when appropriate, reducing burdens for remixers and educators [1][2].

4. Clearer treatment of moral, publicity and privacy rights: the licensor waives or agrees not to assert moral rights (to the extent possible) and clarifies that the license does not affect others’ publicity, privacy or similar rights, giving users better notice of what is and is not granted [3].

5. Automatic reinstatement after breach cure: if a user violates the license, permissions terminate but are automatically reinstated if the violation is corrected within 30 days of discovery, offering a fair pathway to compliance while protecting creators [3].

These enhancements, combined with plainer language, make the 4.0 suite more robust, internationally interoperable and user-friendly than earlier 2.0/3.0 versions [2].

## References  
[1] Creative Commons. “Attribution 4.0 International Deed.” https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/  
[2] Creative Commons. “Version 4.0 Licenses.” https://creativecommons.org/version4  
[3] Creative Commons. “Attribution 4.0 International Legal Code.” https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/legalcode
rounds.answer_b:
The two Attribution-based 4.0 licenses are:

**(a) Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0)**
Canonical URL: **https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/**
This license permits users to **share** (copy and redistribute in any medium or format) and **adapt** (remix, transform, and build upon the material) for **any purpose, including commercial use**. The sole condition is **Attribution**: users must give appropriate credit, provide a link to the license, and indicate if changes were made. There is **no ShareAlike requirement**, so derivative works may be released under different terms [1].

**(b) Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International (CC BY-NC-SA 4.0)**
Canonical URL: **https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/**
This license grants the same share and adapt freedoms but imposes three conditions: **Attribution** (appropriate credit required), **NonCommercial** (no commercial use permitted), and **ShareAlike** (derivatives must be distributed under the same or a compatible license). Additional legal or technological restrictions may not be applied [2].

Both licenses are irrevocable as long as licensees comply with the terms, include no warranties, and note that compliance is unnecessary for public domain elements or where copyright exceptions apply [1][2].

## Major Improvements in the 4.0 Suite

**1. International Enforceability:** The 4.0 licenses are drafted as "International" rather than jurisdiction-specific, eliminating the need for jurisdiction-ported versions. They function globally without reliance on any single country's legal framework [3][4].

**2. Database Rights (Sui Generis):** Version 4.0 explicitly covers sui generis database rights recognized under European and other laws, ensuring that licensing extends to databases and their contents, which prior versions did not clearly address [3][4].

**3. Attribution Flexibility:** The 4.0 suite allows licensors to request removal of attribution information upon request, giving creators more control. Attribution requirements are also more clearly articulated [3][5].

**4. Moral and Publicity Rights:** Version 4.0 explicitly addresses moral rights and publicity/privacy rights, clarifying that these are waived or not asserted only to the limited extent necessary to exercise the licensed rights [3][4].

**5. Automatic Reinstatement Mechanism:** If a licensee violates the terms, the 4.0 licenses provide an automatic reinstatement of rights if the violation is cured within **30 days** of discovery, a feature absent from earlier versions [3][5].

## References
[1] CC BY 4.0 Deed — https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/
[2] CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 Deed — https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/
[3] Creative Commons Wiki: 4.0 License Suite — https://wiki.creativecommons.org/wiki/License_Versions#702
[4] Creative Commons: What's New in 4.0 — https://creativecommons.org/version4/
[5] Creative Commons FAQ: Why should I use the latest version? — https://creativecommons.org/faq/#why-should-i-use-the-latest-version-of-the-creative-commons-licenses
rounds.judge_reasoning:
First, Deep Logic: Both agents correctly identified CC BY 4.0 as the Attribution-only license permitting commercial reuse without ShareAlike, and CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 as the Attribution-based license prohibiting commercial use and requiring ShareAlike. Both referenced official Creative Commons 4.0 documentation and tied improvements to prior versions—so both pass the core entity check.

Width/Completeness: Both provided canonical URLs, accurately summarized permissions and obligations, and explained at least four major 4.0 improvements (internationalization/unported structure, database rights, attribution flexibility, moral/publicity rights clarification, and 30-day automatic reinstatement). Agent A emphasized attribution flexibility via "reasonable manner" and aggregation; Agent B added the licensor’s right to request removal of attribution and mentioned irrevocability and exceptions, slightly increasing informational richness. Both meet the checklist with no significant omissions.

User Experience & Presentation: Agent B is more scannable and user-friendly, with clearer bold section headings, structured condition breakdown (Attribution, NonCommercial, ShareAlike), and cleaner visual hierarchy. It reads more like a polished search-engine answer. Agent A is strong and accurate but slightly denser and less visually optimized.

Conclusion: Both are high-quality and accurate, but Agent B provides a better overall user experience due to superior formatting, clarity, and slightly richer contextual detail—without introducing errors.

lines: -> WINNER is B. Targeting Loser Failure: NONE.

rounds.question: You are preparing to publish a "UNIX / Linux Tutorial for Beginners" and want to release it under the most up-to-date, internationally robust open license version recommended by the nonprofit organization that maintains a suite of widely used public copyright licenses. Identify this nonprofit organization and explain its core mission. Then, compare the three distinct 10-week, sector-specific certificate courses it offers (including their intended audiences and learning focus), and describe the available bootcamp training formats and participation structure. Additionally, outline the developer-oriented tools and open source projects this organization provides to help integrate and manage its licenses in software platforms. Finally, reference the short anniversary video celebrating roughly twenty years of the organization’s work by naming its creators and specifying the license under which the video is released.
checklist_width: Clear explanation of the nonprofit’s mission to support open access to knowledge and a global commons
Description of the Certificate for Educators (focus on open education, OER, open pedagogy, global educator community)
Description of the Certificate for Academic Librarians (focus on open access, institutional policy, equitable publishing, library leadership)
Description of the Certificate for Open Culture (focus on GLAM institutions, digitization, public domain, cultural heritage, indigenous knowledge considerations)

Explanation of bootcamp formats (in-person one-week intensive for ~15–25 participants; online one-week full-day or two-week half-day; 40 hours plus preparatory reading; organized with host institutions)
Mention of developer tools such as the license chooser tool, legal tools management application, platform toolkit, and open source projects supporting discovery/quantification of the commons
Identification of the anniversary video creators (Ryan Junell and Glenn Otis Brown)
Specification that the anniversary video is licensed under CC BY 4.0
checklist_depth: Creative Commons identified as the nonprofit organization + connection to recommending version 4.0 licenses as more internationally robust
Correct identification of all three official certificate tracks (Educators, Academic Librarians, Open Culture) as the sector-specific 10-week programs
Accurate identification of the developer ecosystem (e.g., CC Chooser, CC Legal Tools Application, Quantifying the Commons) as official projects of the same organization
Correct identification of the "Twenty Years" anniversary video and its CC BY 4.0 license as part of the same organization’s work
rounds.rationale: The question uses the logic of selecting the recommended 4.0 open license version (Deep reasoning anchor) to indirectly point to the specific nonprofit behind those licenses without initially naming it. It then requires broad aggregation across multiple areas of that organization’s ecosystem—training certificates, bootcamps, developer tools, mission statement, and a commemorative video—ensuring the respondent must synthesize information from diverse but related sources (Wide scope). The UNIX/Linux tutorial context provides domain relevance while masking the entities behind licensing and training infrastructure.
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Creative Commons (CC) Certificate training develops people’s expertise in CC licenses in order to openly share and engage in our digital commons. With a foundation in open lic
rounds.answer_a:
Creative Commons (CC) is the U.S.–based nonprofit whose mission is to build a globally accessible public commons of knowledge and culture by providing free, standardized copyright licenses and supporting legal-technical tools that enable anyone to share, remix and reuse creative works legally and responsibly [1].  

CC’s professional development program offers three distinct, 10-week Creative Commons Certificate courses:  
• Educators Certificate – designed for K-12 and higher-ed teachers, instructional designers and OER advocates; focuses on copyright fundamentals, open pedagogy and creating/adapting open educational resources [2].  
• Librarians Certificate – aimed at academic, public and special-library staff; emphasizes scholarly communication, open access, metadata and collection development using CC licenses [2].  
• Government Certificate – built for civil servants, policy makers and open data stewards; examines public-sector information, open policy, procurement and data licensing with CC tools [2].  

For those who need an accelerated option, CC runs “Certificate Bootcamps”: intensive, live-online cohorts that compress the same curriculum into one week (five full-day sessions). Participants work in small groups (≈25), complete daily activities, receive instructor feedback, and must submit the same portfolio project to earn the credential [3].  

Developers can integrate CC licensing through several open-source projects:  
• License Chooser (chooser.creativecommons.org) – embeddable React app and API that outputs machine-readable RDFa/HTML for selected CC licenses [4].  
• CC REL and vocabularies – RDF schema and JSON-LD contexts that standardize metadata for CC works [5].  
• CC Catalog/Openverse API – open dataset and REST API supplying >700 million CC-licensed images for search and reuse [6].  
• cc-engine, cc-legal-tools & draft-compatibility libraries – Python packages that parse, verify and display license metadata inside software platforms or CMS plugins [7].  
All code is maintained publicly on Creative Commons’ GitHub under permissive licenses (mainly MIT or Apache-2.0).  

To mark roughly twenty years of CC, the organization released the 1½-minute animation “Better Sharing, Brighter Future.” The film was created by Creative Commons in collaboration with animation studio Thought Café and is itself licensed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0) [8].  

## References  
[1] Creative Commons – About. https://creativecommons.org/about/  
[2] Creative Commons Certificate – Overview. https://certificates.creativecommons.org/  
[3] CC Certificate Bootcamp FAQ. https://certificates.creativecommons.org/bootcamps/  
[4] Creative Commons License Chooser GitHub repository. https://github.com/creativecommons/chooser  
[5] CC Rights Expression Language (CC REL). https://creativecommons.org/ns  
[6] Openverse API Docs (formerly CC Catalog). https://api.openverse.engineering/v1/  
[7] Creative Commons Open Source Projects Index. https://creativecommons.github.io/  
[8] “Better Sharing, Brighter Future – 20 Years of Creative Commons” video. https://creativecommons.org/2021/12/06/better-sharing-brighter-future/
rounds.answer_b:
**Creative Commons** is the nonprofit organization that maintains the widely used suite of public copyright licenses, including the recommended CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 International license for open educational works [1]. Its core mission is to build a globally accessible commons of knowledge and culture by providing free, standardized legal tools that allow creators to share their work with clear permissions, reducing barriers to creativity, education, and innovation worldwide [1][2].

## Certificate Courses

Creative Commons offers three distinct **10-week, sector-specific CC Certificate courses** [3]:

1. **CC for Educators** — Designed for teachers, instructional designers, and academic professionals. It focuses on finding, using, and creating Open Educational Resources (OER), understanding open pedagogy, and applying CC licenses in educational settings [3].

2. **CC for Librarians** — Tailored for library professionals. It covers how CC licenses support open access, digital collections management, copyright literacy, and advocacy for open policies within library institutions [3].

3. **CC for Government** — Aimed at public sector professionals. It addresses the application of open licensing to publicly funded works, open data policies, and increasing public access to government resources [3].

## Bootcamp Training

Creative Commons also offers an intensive **bootcamp format** — a condensed, facilitator-led version of the certificate training designed for cohorts from specific institutions or organizations. Participants engage in structured group learning with collaborative activities, typically completed over a shorter compressed timeframe compared to the 10-week course [3][4].

## Developer Tools and Open Source Projects

Creative Commons provides several developer-oriented tools and open source projects. **CC Chooser** helps users select the appropriate license for their work. **CC Search** (now integrated into **Openverse**) enables discovery of openly licensed content across platforms. The **CC Legal Code** repository on GitHub maintains machine-readable license metadata. Additionally, CC develops **CC REL** (Rights Expression Language) vocabulary and structured data tools, and various **API integrations** that help software platforms embed and manage CC license information programmatically [5][6].

## Anniversary Video

To celebrate roughly twenty years of its work, Creative Commons released a short anniversary video created by **Ryan Junell and Glenn Otis Brown**, titled *"A Shared Culture."* This video is itself released under a **Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike (CC BY-SA)** license, exemplifying the organization's commitment to openness [7][8].

## References
[1] Creative Commons — About — https://creativecommons.org/about/
[2] Creative Commons Mission — https://creativecommons.org/mission
[3] CC Certificate Program — https://certificates.creativecommons.org/
[4] CC Certificate Bootcamp — https://certificates.creativecommons.org/bootcamp
[5] CC Developer Tools — https://opensource.creativecommons.org/
[6] Openverse (formerly CC Search) — https://openverse.org/
[7] "A Shared Culture" Video — https://creativecommons.org/about/videos/a-shared-culture/
[8] Creative Commons 20th Anniversary — https://creativecommons.org/2021/12/17/celebrating-20-years/
rounds.judge_reasoning:
First, Deep Logic: Both agents correctly identify Creative Commons as the nonprofit and connect it to the internationally robust 4.0 license suite. So both pass the core entity check.

Width/Completeness: Both fail key checklist items. Critically, both incorrectly list “Government” as one of the three 10-week certificate tracks, instead of the required Open Culture certificate (alongside Educators and Academic Librarians). Both also omit important bootcamp specifics (in-person option, 40-hour structure, host institutions, participant size range) and fail to clearly mention projects like the Legal Tools Application and Quantifying the Commons.

Anniversary Video: Agent A gets the license correct (CC BY 4.0) but incorrectly names the creators. Agent B correctly names Ryan Junell and Glenn Otis Brown, but gives the wrong license (CC BY-SA instead of CC BY 4.0). Thus, both contain factual errors on this required element.

User Experience & Presentation: Both are well-structured and readable, with clear headings and good scannability. Agent A provides slightly more technical depth on developer tools, but Agent B is cleaner and more directly aligned with the question’s structure. However, since both contain major factual inaccuracies across multiple required checklist items, neither can be considered high quality.

Conclusion: Because both agents pass Deep Logic but fail multiple mandatory checklist elements (certificates, bootcamp detail depth, anniversary specifics), this is a LOW-quality tie.

rounds.question:
An author of a beginner-friendly UNIX/Linux tutorial wants to release their material under a Creative Commons 4.0 license. In one scenario, they want to allow anyone to use, modify, and redistribute the work for any purpose, including commercially, as long as proper credit is given and no additional legal restrictions are imposed. In another scenario, they want to allow sharing and adaptation, but prohibit commercial use and require that derivative works be distributed under the same license terms. 

Identify the two specific Creative Commons 4.0 licenses that satisfy these two different goals. For each license, provide: (1) its canonical URL, (2) the core permissions granted, (3) the key conditions or restrictions imposed, and (4) a summary of important improvements introduced in the 4.0 license suite that make these licenses more internationally robust and user-friendly compared to earlier versions (such as changes related to global enforceability, database rights, attribution practices, moral rights, and reinstatement after violations).
checklist_width: Canonical URL for Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0)
Permissions under CC BY 4.0: sharing and adapting for any purpose, including commercial use
Conditions under CC BY 4.0: attribution and no additional restrictions
Canonical URL for Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International (CC BY-NC-SA 4.0)
Permissions under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0: sharing and adapting
Conditions under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0: attribution, noncommercial restriction, share-alike requirement, no additional restrictions
Explanation of 4.0 improvements: international enforceability without porting
Explanation of 4.0 improvements: explicit coverage of sui generis database rights
Explanation of 4.0 improvements: clarified and flexible attribution (e.g., linking to attribution page)
Explanation of 4.0 improvements: waiver/handling of moral, publicity, and personality rights
Explanation of 4.0 improvements: 30-day automatic reinstatement after curing violations
checklist_depth: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0) + Logic: allows commercial use with only attribution and no additional restrictions
Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International (CC BY-NC-SA 4.0) + Logic: prohibits commercial use and requires derivatives to be licensed under identical terms
rounds.rationale: The question anchors to a UNIX/Linux beginner tutorial context but does not reference any specific document. It uses logical conditions (commercial vs. noncommercial + share-alike) to force identification of the correct 4.0 licenses (Deep reasoning). It then requires aggregation of canonical URLs, permissions, restrictions, and multiple cross-cutting improvements introduced in version 4.0, ensuring comparison across both licenses and the broader 4.0 suite (Wide aggregation).
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Canonical URL https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/ See the legal code You are free to: Share — copy and redistribute the material in any medium or format for any purpo
rounds.answer_a:
Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0)  
1. Canonical URL: https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/ [1]  
2. Core permissions: anyone worldwide may copy, share, display, perform, translate, adapt, remix, transform, and build upon the work for any purpose, including commercially, for the full term of copyright [1].  
3. Key conditions: users must give appropriate credit, provide the license link, and indicate changes; they may not apply legal terms or technological measures (e.g., DRM) that restrict others from exercising the licensed freedoms [1]. No Non-Commercial or ShareAlike requirement applies.  
4. 4.0-suite improvements:  
   • Single global text replaces jurisdiction-specific “ported” versions, improving cross-border enforceability [3].  
   • Explicit grant of any sui generis database rights owned by the licensor, eliminating a separate permission layer for data reuse [3].  
   • Attribution can be supplied “in any reasonable manner,” including via hyperlink or consolidated credit lists, easing compliance for digital and educational uses [1][3].  
   • Licensor waives or agrees not to assert moral rights to the extent allowed and clarifies that privacy and publicity rights are unaffected, giving clearer scope of permission [3].  
   • Automatic reinstatement: permissions automatically resume if a violator cures the breach within 30 days of discovery, encouraging good-faith compliance while protecting creators [3].

Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International (CC BY-NC-SA 4.0)  
1. Canonical URL: https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/ [2]  
2. Core permissions: anyone may copy, share, and adapt the material, including creating remixes and translations, for non-commercial purposes only [2].  
3. Key conditions:  
   • Attribution—same as above;  
   • NonCommercial—no use “primarily intended for or directed toward commercial advantage or monetary compensation” [2];  
   • ShareAlike—adaptations must be licensed under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 or a compatible license [2];  
   • No additional legal/technological restrictions may be imposed.  
4. 4.0-suite improvements (all of the CC BY 4.0 points apply) plus:  
   • Compatibility mechanism enables future designation of licenses with equivalent NC-SA provisions, broadening remix possibilities across communities [3].  
   • Clearer definition of “NonCommercial,” providing greater certainty for educators and non-profits [2].  

These enhancements make both licenses more internationally robust, clearer for licensors and users, and better adapted to modern digital and data-driven scholarship than earlier 2.0/3.0 editions.

## References  
[1] Creative Commons. “Attribution 4.0 International Deed.” https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/  
[2] Creative Commons. “Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International Deed.” https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/  
[3] Creative Commons. “Version 4.0 Licenses – Explained.” https://creativecommons.org/version4
rounds.answer_b:
**Scenario 1: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0)**

**Canonical URL:** https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/ [1]

**Core Permissions:** Users may **Share** (copy and redistribute in any medium or format) and **Adapt** (remix, transform, and build upon the material) for any purpose, including commercially [1].

**Key Conditions:** The sole condition is **Attribution** — users must give appropriate credit, provide a link to the license, and indicate if changes were made, without implying licensor endorsement. Users may not apply additional legal terms or technological measures that restrict others from exercising licensed rights. No ShareAlike requirement exists, so derivatives may use different terms [1][2].

---

**Scenario 2: Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International (CC BY-NC-SA 4.0)**

**Canonical URL:** https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/ [3]

**Core Permissions:** Users may **Share** and **Adapt** the material under specified conditions [3].

**Key Conditions:** Three conditions apply: **Attribution** (same as above), **NonCommercial** (material cannot be used for commercial purposes), and **ShareAlike** (derivative works must be distributed under the same or a compatible license). Additional legal or technological restrictions are prohibited. Both licenses are irrevocable while terms are followed, require no compliance for public domain elements or fair use situations, and include no warranties [3][4].

---

**Major 4.0 Suite Improvements**

**Global Enforceability:** The 4.0 licenses are titled "International" rather than being jurisdiction-ported, functioning uniformly worldwide without dependence on any single legal system [5][6].

**Database Rights:** Version 4.0 explicitly covers sui generis database rights recognized in European and other jurisdictions, closing a gap left by earlier versions [5][6].

**Attribution Flexibility:** Licensors may request removal of attribution information, and attribution requirements are more clearly articulated than in prior versions [5][7].

**Moral and Publicity Rights:** The 4.0 suite expressly addresses moral rights and publicity/privacy rights, clarifying they are waived only to the limited extent necessary for exercising licensed rights [5][6].

**Automatic Reinstatement:** If a licensee violates terms but cures the violation within **30 days** of discovery, rights are automatically reinstated — a mechanism absent from earlier versions [5][7].

## References
[1] CC BY 4.0 Deed — https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/
[2] CC BY 4.0 Legal Code — https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/legalcode
[3] CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 Deed — https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/
[4] CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 Legal Code — https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/legalcode
[5] Creative Commons Wiki: License Versions — https://wiki.creativecommons.org/wiki/License_Versions
[6] Creative Commons: What's New in 4.0 — https://creativecommons.org/version4/
[7] Creative Commons FAQ — https://creativecommons.org/faq/#why-should-i-use-the-latest-version-of-the-creative-commons-licenses
rounds.judge_reasoning:
First, Deep Logic: Both Agent A and Agent B correctly identified CC BY 4.0 for the commercial-use-with-attribution scenario and CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 for the noncommercial + share-alike scenario. Both pass the core entity check.

Width/Completeness: Both agents provided canonical URLs, core permissions, key conditions, and covered all required 4.0 improvements: international (non-ported) enforceability, sui generis database rights, flexible attribution practices, handling/waiver of moral and publicity rights, and 30-day automatic reinstatement. Neither omitted any major checklist item, and both explanations are accurate.

User Experience & Presentation: Agent B is more scannable and user-friendly. It uses clearer bold section headers, consistent structure across scenarios, and strong visual separation (horizontal rules). The permissions and conditions are presented in a way that allows a reader to find the answer in seconds (BLUF-friendly). It also provides slightly richer contextual grounding (e.g., irrevocability, no warranties) and higher citation density. Agent A is accurate and solid but more text-dense and slightly less polished in presentation.

Conclusion: Since both are factually strong and complete, the decision rests on formatting, clarity, and overall user experience. Agent B provides the better structured and more immediately usable response, making it the style winner.
